skill.name: log-analysis
skill.description: Analyze application logs to identify errors, performance issues, and security anomalies. Use when debugging issues, monitoring system health, or investigating incidents. Handles various log formats including Apache, Nginx, application logs, and JSON logs.
skill.summary: Analyze application logs to identify errors, performance issues, and security anomalies
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All 25 static findings are false positives. The skill provides documentation with example bash commands (grep, awk) in markdown code blocks. Hardcoded URLs point to legitimate documentation (GNU grep/awk manuals). The IP address 192.168.1.100 is an example in private range. No cryptographic code exists. This is a safe, read-only log analysis skill.

medium_findings.title: External Command Examples in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill contains 19 instances of bash command examples (grep, awk, tail) in markdown code blocks. These are documentation/examples for users, not executable code. No user input is passed to shell commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 'external_commands' detections are bash examples in markdown code blocks - documentation only, not executable code

medium_findings.title: Documentation Reference URLs
medium_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to legitimate documentation sites (gnu.org, loggly.com). These are safe external references for user learning.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are standard documentation links to GNU grep, GNU awk, and Loggly - all reputable sources

medium_findings.title: Example IP Address in Command
medium_findings.description: The IP 192.168.1.100 appears in an example grep command. This is a private IP range address used as a teaching example.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: 192.168.x.x is a private IP range; this is clearly an example, not actual network code

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers and DevOps engineers quickly identify errors, performance bottlenecks, and security threats in application logs using proven command-line techniques.
actual_capabilities: Search and filter log files for error patterns using grep and awk
Analyze HTTP access logs to identify 4xx and 5xx errors
Detect security threats like SQL injection, XSS, and brute force attempts
Perform time-based analysis to find error bursts and incidents
Extract and analyze response times for performance troubleshooting
Generate structured analysis reports with recommendations
limitations: Does not parse binary or proprietary log formats
Cannot analyze logs on remote systems without file access
Requires manual interpretation of analysis results
Does not provide real-time alerting or automated responses

use_cases.title: Debug Production Incidents
use_cases.description: Quickly find root causes during outages by searching error patterns and analyzing timestamps

use_cases.title: Security Incident Investigation
use_cases.description: Detect suspicious patterns like SQL injection, XSS attempts, and brute force attacks in access logs

use_cases.title: Performance Optimization
use_cases.description: Identify slow requests and traffic patterns to optimize application performance

prompt_templates.title: Find Recent Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the log-analysis skill to find the last 50 error messages in /var/log/application.log and summarize the error types found.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze HTTP Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the log-analysis skill to analyze access.log and identify all 5xx server errors in the last 24 hours. Group by error code and show count.

prompt_templates.title: Security Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the log-analysis skill to scan access.log for SQL injection attempts, XSS attempts, and directory traversal patterns. Report suspicious IPs.

prompt_templates.title: Performance Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the log-analysis skill to analyze access.log and find the top 20 slowest requests. Show response times and URLs.

output_examples.output:
## Log Analysis Report

### Summary
- Analysis window: Last 100 lines
- Error count: 12
- Warning count: 5

### Error Types Found
| Error Type | Count | Last Seen |
|------------|-------|----------|
| Database connection timeout | 5 | 14:32 |
| NullPointerException | 4 | 14:28 |
| File not found | 3 | 14:15 |

### Recommended Actions
1. Check database connection pool settings
2. Review recent deployments for null handling issues
3. Verify file path configurations

output_examples.input: Scan for SQL injection in access.log
output_examples.output:
## Security Analysis Report

### Threats Detected
- SQL injection attempts: 3
- Suspicious IPs: 2

### Details
- 192.168.1.50: 3 UNION SELECT attempts at 14:20
- 192.168.1.51: 2 DROP TABLE attempts at 14:25

### Recommendations
1. Block suspicious IPs at firewall level
2. Review WAF rules for SQL injection patterns
3. Enable enhanced logging for login endpoints

best_practices: Always set a clear time window for analysis to reduce noise
Use context flags (-B, -A) with grep to see lines before and after matches
Save commonly used grep patterns as scripts for repeatable analysis
anti_patterns: Do not analyze full logs without time filters - this produces overwhelming output
Do not ignore warning messages - they often precede errors
Do not forget to mask sensitive data when sharing analysis results

faq.question: What log formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill works with plain text logs including Apache, Nginx, JSON logs, and application logs in standard formats.

faq.question: Can I analyze remote logs with this skill?
faq.answer: No, you need local file access to log files. Use SCP or SSH to copy logs locally first.

faq.question: Does this skill modify log files?
faq.answer: No, this skill only reads and analyzes logs. It never modifies or deletes any data.

faq.question: How do I analyze compressed log files?
faq.answer: Use zgrep, zawk, or zcat commands to analyze compressed (.gz) log files directly.

faq.question: Can this skill detect all security threats?
faq.answer: It detects common patterns like SQL injection and XSS, but sophisticated attacks may require specialized security tools.

faq.question: What tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: It primarily uses grep, awk, and tail - standard Unix command-line tools for log analysis.
